pros: Hybrid model unifying instruct, reasoning, and coding in a single efficient model
Very cost-effective pricing
Multimodal (text + image input)
Open-weight (Apache 2.0)
Multilingual support
Good for high-volume production workloads
cons: Less capable than larger frontier models for complex reasoning
May not match Mistral Medium/Large on difficult tasks
mistral-small-latest.special_features: Hybrid reasoning model — can toggle between standard and thinking modes. Supports structured outputs and function calling. Open weights under Apache 2.0.
mistral-small-latest.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 32K token context (app config), but Mistral docs show 128K context window for the latest version.

pros: Frontier-class multimodal model optimized for agentic and coding use cases
State-of-the-art performance
Simplified enterprise deployments
Cost-efficient for its capability tier
Strong at agentic workflows and tool use
mistral-medium-latest.special_features: Mistral's flagship frontier model. Optimized for agentic use cases and coding. Multimodal (text + image). Supports function calling, structured outputs, and JSON mode. Latest featured model on Mistral's platform.
mistral-medium-latest.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 32K token context (app config), but Mistral docs show 128K context window.

pros: Large 128K context window
Very affordable (same input/output pricing)
Open-weight model (Apache 2.0)
Good for code tasks
Multilingual with Tekken tokenizer supporting 100+ languages
cons: Now listed as legacy/deprecated by Mistral (retirement 7/31/2026, replacement: Ministral 3 8B)
Smaller model — less capable than frontier models
open-mistral-nemo.special_features: Built in collaboration with NVIDIA. Uses the Tekken tokenizer for efficient multilingual encoding. Designed for code tasks. Part of the open-weight lineup.
open-mistral-nemo.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 128K token context. Marked as legacy on Mistral docs with deprecation date 5/22/2026 and retirement 7/31/2026.

pros: Specialized for code generation, completion, and fill-in-the-middle (FIM)
Large 256K context window
Low latency — optimized for high-frequency code completion
Supports 80+ programming languages
Excellent for IDE integrations and coding assistants
cons: Specialized model — not a general-purpose chat model
Premier license (not open-weight; commercial use restricted)
codestral-latest.special_features: Cutting-edge code generation model. Supports fill-in-the-middle (FIM), code completion, and instruction following. Trained on 80+ programming languages. Premier-tier model. Has a dedicated Codestral API endpoint separate from the chat API.
codestral-latest.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 256K token context. Latest version is v25.08. Previous Codestral v25.01 was deprecated 11/6/2025.

pros: Very lightweight and efficient — runs on edge devices
Best-in-class text and vision capabilities for its size
Extremely cost-effective (same input/output price)
Open-weight
128K context window
Good for on-device / edge AI applications
cons: Small model — limited on complex reasoning and difficult tasks
Original Ministral 8B (v24.1) deprecated 12/31/2025; replaced by Ministral 3 8B
ministral-8b-latest.special_features: Part of the Ministral 3 family — edge-optimized models with best-in-class text and vision. Supports agentic workflows. Designed for low-latency, resource-constrained environments. Multimodal (text + image).
ministral-8b-latest.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 128K token context. The 'ministral-8b-latest' alias now points to Ministral 3 8B (v25.12).

command-a-03-2025.parameter_count: Not publicly disclosed (described as efficient — runs on only 2 GPUs)
pros: Cohere's most performant model to date
Excellent at tool use, agents, and RAG (retrieval augmented generation)
Large 256K context window
Strong multilingual capabilities
150% higher throughput compared to Command R+ 08-2024
Only requires two GPUs to run — cost-efficient deployment
Built-in web search capability via Cohere's connectors
cons: No vision/image input (use Command A Vision for that)
No dedicated reasoning/thinking mode (use Command A Reasoning for that)
command-a-03-2025.special_features: Flagship Command model. Excels at agentic workflows, RAG, and multilingual tasks. 256K context. Supports tool use and structured outputs. Cohere also offers Command A Reasoning (reasoning variant, 256K context, 32K output) and Command A Vision (image input, 128K context) as separate models.
command-a-03-2025.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 256K token context. Pricing based on Cohere's standard Command A pricing tier.

pros: Small, fast, and cost-effective
Excels at RAG, tool use, and agents
Good for complex reasoning and multi-step tasks despite small size
128K context window
Lowest-priced Cohere Command model
cons: Small model — less capable than Command A or R+ on complex tasks
command-r7b-12-2024.special_features: Compact model designed for speed and efficiency. Good at RAG and agentic workflows. Supports tool use and structured outputs. Ideal for high-throughput, low-latency applications.
command-r7b-12-2024.license: Proprietary (CC-BY-NC-4.0 for weights, commercial API available)
command-r7b-12-2024.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 128K token context.

pros: Best suited for complex RAG workflows and multi-step tool use
Strong instruction-following and conversational capabilities
128K context window
Good multilingual support
Higher quality than Command R and R7B
cons: Superseded by Command A (which has 150% higher throughput and 256K context)
command-r-plus-08-2024.special_features: Instruction-following conversational model optimized for RAG and multi-step tool use. Supports structured outputs. Predecessor to Command A. Part of the Command R family.
command-r-plus-08-2024.license: Proprietary (CC-BY-NC-4.0 for weights, commercial API available)
command-r-plus-08-2024.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 128K token context. Update of the original Command R+ (04-2024).

pricing.input_per_million: Free on SambaNova developer tier; ~$0.50-$1.00 estimated for paid tiers
pricing.output_per_million: Free on SambaNova developer tier; ~$1.00-$2.00 estimated for paid tiers

pros: Open-weight model from Meta — widely adopted and well-supported
70B parameters — strong general-purpose capabilities
128K context window on SambaNova
Excellent instruction following
Good at code generation and reasoning tasks
SambaNova's RDU architecture provides fast inference
Available free on SambaNova's developer tier
cons: No dedicated reasoning/thinking mode (standard instruct model)
No built-in web search (must use external tools)
Llama 3.x is now older generation compared to Llama 4
Meta-Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct.special_features: Hosted on SambaNova's RDU (Reconfigurable Dataflow Unit) infrastructure for high-speed inference. Open-weight model. Supports tool calling and structured outputs. Part of Meta's Llama 3.3 family — matches Llama 3.1 405B quality at 70B size. Available on SambaNova Cloud.
Meta-Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 131,072 token context. SambaNova docs confirm 128K context, text-only modality.

glm-5p2.developer: Zhipu AI / Z.AI (hosted on Fireworks AI)
glm-5p2.parameter_count: Not publicly disclosed (large MoE, estimated 300B+ range)
pros: Opus-level intelligence at open-source rates (per Fireworks banner)
Strong reasoning/thinking capabilities with reasoning_content field
Supports interleaved thinking for multi-step agentic workflows
Supports preserved thinking across conversation turns
Tool calling and structured outputs
Multiple serving tiers (Standard, Priority, Fast)
Prompt caching at 50% discount on cached input tokens
cons: Can be expensive at Priority/Fast tiers
No built-in web search (must use tools)
Can produce very long reasoning traces (need to set max_tokens)
glm-5p2.special_features: Reasoning model with thinking/reasoning_content support. Supports reasoning_effort parameter (low/medium/high) and Anthropic-compatible thinking parameter. Supports interleaved thinking (reasoning between tool calls) and preserved thinking (reasoning retained across turns). Featured on Fireworks AI homepage. Uses GLM (General Language Model) architecture from Zhipu AI / Z.AI.
glm-5p2.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 131,072 token context, style='reasoning'. This is the model this Hermes instance is running on. Previous version GLM 5.1 also available on Fireworks.

kimi-k2p7-code.developer: Moonshot AI / Kimi (hosted on Fireworks AI)
kimi-k2p7-code.parameter_count: ~1T (MoE architecture, part of Kimi K2 family which is >300B for fine-tuning tier)
pros: Specialized for code generation and agentic coding workflows
Part of the Kimi K2 family — strong agentic capabilities
Excellent tool-calling performance
Multiple serving tiers available
Good for coding agents in terminal, IDE, and background
cons: Limited to 32K context window (smallest context in the registry)
Can produce very long output — must set max_tokens explicitly
Inference can be slow on large inputs (10-30 min timeout recommended for agentic loops)
kimi-k2p7-code.special_features: Code-specialized variant of the Kimi K2 family. Optimized for agentic coding workflows. Fireworks recommends setting max_tokens explicitly (256-512 for tool calls, 512-1024 for short text, 1024-2048 for JSON, 4096+ for long-form). Tool schemas should have clearly distinct names and descriptions. Part of the Kimi K2 family that includes K2.5, K2.6, and K2.7 variants.
kimi-k2p7-code.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 32,768 token context, style='direct'. Part of Kimi K2 family on Fireworks.

pros: Reasoning/thinking model at a very affordable price point
Lowest-priced reasoning model in the registry
Supports reasoning_effort and thinking parameters
Good value for multimodal reasoning tasks
Part of the popular Qwen model family
cons: Extremely limited context window — only 4,000 tokens (smallest in registry)
No Priority or Fast serving tier available
No vision/image input despite being described as 'multimodal reasoning'
May struggle with long documents or extended conversations
qwen3p7-plus.special_features: Reasoning model from the Qwen 3.7 Plus generation. Supports reasoning_content field, reasoning_effort parameter, and Anthropic-compatible thinking parameter. Very cost-effective for reasoning tasks. Previous version Qwen 3.6 Plus also available ($0.50/$0.10/$3.00).
qwen3p7-plus.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 4,000 token context (very limited), style='reasoning'. Described as 'multimodal reasoning' in app config but text-only on Fireworks.

deepseek-v4-pro.parameter_count: Not publicly disclosed (large MoE, >300B for fine-tuning tier, likely 600B+ range)
pros: Deep reasoning capabilities with thinking/reasoning_content support
Large 131K context window
Strong code generation and general reasoning
Supports interleaved and preserved thinking
DeepSeek models known for excellent price/performance ratio
Prompt caching at significant discount (~8% of input price)
cons: Can produce very long reasoning traces
deepseek-v4-pro.special_features: Deep reasoning model from DeepSeek's V4 Pro generation. Supports reasoning_effort, thinking parameter, interleaved thinking, and preserved thinking. Part of the DeepSeek V4 family which also includes DeepSeek V4 Flash (a faster, cheaper variant at $0.14/$0.028/$0.28). MoE architecture. Known for strong coding and mathematical reasoning.
deepseek-v4-pro.notes: Listed in AICookies registry with 131,072 token context, style='reasoning'. DeepSeek V4 Flash (cheaper variant) also available on Fireworks at $0.14 input / $0.028 cached / $0.28 output per million tokens.
